initial_program:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Border coordinates
% --------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Unique wall colour (the only nonzero colour on the border)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% exactly one wall colour
1 { Wall : color(W) } 1 :- boundary_color(W).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. 4connected moves
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Floodfill for the outside region (cells reachable from the border
%    through cells that are NOT the wall colour)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
    (R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin)).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
    (R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax)).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
    (C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin)).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
    (C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax)).
outside(R,C) :- border_cell(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Decompose each output cell into (anchor cell) and (offset)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
anchor_row(R,BR,DR) :- R = BR * NR + DR, DR >= 0, DR < NR.
anchor_col(C,BC,DC) :- C = BC * NC + DC, DC >= 0, DC < NC.

output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC) :-
    anchor_row(R,BR,DR),
    anchor_col(C,BC,DC),
    input(BR,BC,_), input(DR,DC,_).

output_cell(R,C) :- output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Determine the colour of each output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% (a) the offset cell itself is the wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,Wall).

% (b) offset is a nonwall cell but reachable from the border  colour 0
output(R,C,0) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    outside(DR,DC).

% (c) offset is a nonwall cell NOT reachable from the border  colour Wall
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    not outside(DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Output size must be N*N by N*N (input is square)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
R < NR * NR.
C < NC * NC.
R >= 0.
C >= 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Enumerate each output cell and choose a colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

rounds.program_after:
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Border coordinates
% --------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Unique wall colour (the only nonzero colour on the border)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% exactly one wall colour
1 { Wall : color(Wall) } 1 :- boundary_color(Wall).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. 4connected moves
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Floodfill for the outside region (cells reachable from the border
%    through cells that are NOT the wall colour)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).
outside(R,C) :- border_cell(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Decompose each output cell into (anchor cell) and (offset)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
anchor_row(R,BR,DR) :- R = BR * NR + DR, DR >= 0, DR < NR.
anchor_col(C,BC,DC) :- C = BC * NC + DC, DC >= 0, DC < NC.

output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC) :-
    anchor_row(R,BR,DR),
    anchor_col(C,BC,DC),
    input(BR,BC,_), input(DR,DC,_).

output_cell(R,C) :- output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Determine the colour of each output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% (a) the offset cell itself is the wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,Wall).

% (b) offset is a nonwall cell but reachable from the border  colour 0
output(R,C,0) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    outside(DR,DC).

% (c) offset is a nonwall cell NOT reachable from the border  colour Wall
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    not outside(DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Output size must be N*N by N*N (input is square)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
R < NR * NR.
C < NC * NC.
R >= 0.
C >= 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Enumerate each output cell and choose a colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Grid dimensions
% --------------------------------------------------------------
n_rows(NR) :- NR = #count{R : input(R,_,_) }.
n_cols(NC) :- NC = #count{C : input(_,C,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Border coordinates
% --------------------------------------------------------------
min_row(Rmin) :- Rmin = #min{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
max_row(Rmax) :- Rmax = #max{Row : input(Row,_,_) }.
min_col(Cmin) :- Cmin = #min{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.
max_col(Cmax) :- Cmax = #max{Col : input(_,Col,_) }.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Unique wall colour (the only nonzero colour on the border)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin).
boundary_color(W) :-
    input(R,C,V), V = W, V != 0, C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).

% exactly one wall colour
1 { Wall : color(Wall) } 1 :- boundary_color(Wall).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. 4connected moves
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Floodfill for the outside region (cells reachable from the border
%    through cells that are NOT the wall colour)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
R = Rmin, min_row(Rmin).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
R = Rmax, max_row(Rmax).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
C = Cmin, min_col(Cmin).
border_cell(R,C) :-
    input(R,C,V), V != Wall,
C = Cmax, max_col(Cmax).
outside(R,C) :- border_cell(R,C).
outside(R2,C2) :-
    outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. Decompose each output cell into (anchor cell) and (offset)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
anchor_row(R,BR,DR) :- R = BR * NR + DR, DR >= 0, DR < NR.
anchor_col(C,BC,DC) :- C = BC * NC + DC, DC >= 0, DC < NC.

output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC) :-
    anchor_row(R,BR,DR),
    anchor_col(C,BC,DC),
    input(BR,BC,_), input(DR,DC,_).

output_cell(R,C) :- output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 7. Determine the colour of each output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% (a) the offset cell itself is the wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,Wall).

% (b) offset is a nonwall cell but reachable from the border  colour 0
output(R,C,0) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    outside(DR,DC).

% (c) offset is a nonwall cell NOT reachable from the border  colour Wall
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    output_pos(R,C,BR,BC,DR,DC),
    input(DR,DC,V), V != Wall,
    not outside(DR,DC).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 8. Exactly one colour per output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,C1), output(R,C,C2), C1 != C2 :- false.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 9. Output size must be N*N by N*N (input is square)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
R < NR * NR.
C < NC * NC.
R >= 0.
C >= 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Enumerate each output cell and choose a colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
